headline: Orcadian pitches a UK North Sea Gigagrid — offshore power with integrated CO2 capture and reinjection to run a data centre
summary: Orcadian Energy began the Assessment Phase for the Earlham and Orwell gas fields with a preferred concept of a ~300 MW offshore power station with integrated carbon capture (designed with GE Vernova and Capsol Technologies), fuelled by the fields' high-CO2 gas, reinjecting the captured CO2 into the reservoir and feeding low-carbon power to a co-located offshore data centre. Orcadian farmed out 50% of the licence P2680 sub-area to the Marine Low Carbon Power Company (MLCP).
co2crc_note: Generate-power-and-reinject-CO2 as the monetisation route for high-CO2 gas is directly analogous to Australian sour-gas fields and to reservoir-reinjection concepts CO2CRC studies.
extractor_note: Early-stage development concept plus a 50% farm-out; classed R&D/announced. GE Vernova supplies the turbines and Capsol the capture tech; org_types are best-fit. Power rating 300 MW, not a CO2 capacity, so capacity_mtpa null.

headline: Industry groups urge Baltic-bordering governments to clear the barriers to offshore CO2 storage
summary: An alliance of industry stakeholders called on Baltic Sea littoral-state governments to address obstacles still blocking offshore CO2 storage, with the Helsinki Convention (HELCOM) singled out as the remaining legal barrier (unlike the London Protocol and OSPAR elsewhere). An independent review of the legality, environmental impact and technical feasibility of Baltic CCS has been flagged.
co2crc_note: Regional-sea-convention barriers to sub-seabed storage (HELCOM here) parallel the London Protocol / Sea Dumping Act questions that govern Australian offshore and cross-border CO2 storage.
extractor_note: Advocacy/regulatory item, no financial dimension -> commitment_status na. Specific industry bodies not named in accessible text; HELCOM captured as the named convention body.

headline: IOGP Europe warns the EU that targets don't build projects — enabling measures, not just storage targets, are what CCS needs
summary: IOGP Europe's Caterina De Matteis argued the EU's Net-Zero Industry Act CO2-injection targets will not deliver without commercially viable frameworks, and that the forthcoming CO2 markets and infrastructure legislative proposal must close the developer-vs-emitter commitment gap. An industry-body positioning piece ahead of the EU's key CCS legislative package.
co2crc_note: The developer-builds-storage-but-emitters-won't-commit gap is a live risk for Australian storage hubs too; IOGP's framing informs how CO2CRC argues the policy case.
extractor_note: Industry-body advocacy on pending EU legislation; placed in policy (not media) as substantive policy commentary. target_year 2030 refers to the NZIA injection-capacity objective referenced.

headline: Study maps calcium-looping direct air capture as a route to cut cement-manufacturing emissions
summary: A peer-reviewed analysis in Joule assesses calcium-looping direct air capture for reducing cement-manufacturing emissions, exploiting the synergy between the CaCO3/CaO sorbent chemistry of DAC and the cement kiln, as a pathway toward net-negative cement by cycling sorbent through the DAC unit before calcination.
co2crc_note: DAC-plus-cement integration is an emerging capture pathway relevant to hard-to-abate sectors CO2CRC tracks, though not tied to an Australian project.
extractor_note: Peer-reviewed science item (Tier-4, allowed in tech section as it cites/is primary research). No project or money dimension -> na.

headline: Bioforcetech turns sewage sludge into carbon-negative concrete filler and destroys PFAS in the process
summary: Californian firm Bioforcetech's OurCarbon process converts municipal sewage sludge into a CO2-negative concrete ingredient while breaking down PFAS forever chemicals and diverting organics from methane-emitting landfill. It won a US$310,000 Bridge Carbontech grant from Columbia University's Carbontech Development Initiative to build a dosing system for existing concrete batch plants.
co2crc_note: A durable CO2-utilisation-in-concrete pathway with a waste-management co-benefit; peripheral to CO2CRC's geological-storage focus but part of the CDR toolkit.
extractor_note: Grant of US$310k awarded (allocated). Value chain best-fit utilisation + mineralisation (CO2 locked into a concrete filler).

headline: Entergy and MHI Group sign an MOU to cut integrated gas-turbine-plus-CCS costs by ~50%
summary: US utility Entergy and Mitsubishi Heavy Industries (MHIA / Mitsubishi Power Americas) signed an MOU to develop a standardised, integrated gas-turbine-combined-cycle-plus-carbon-capture offering, targeting a ~50% reduction in overall project costs; Entergy sits near the largest existing US CO2 pipeline network and favourable storage geology.
co2crc_note: A cost-down MOU for power-plant CCS from a major turbine OEM signals the technology-standardisation trend CO2CRC watches, but no Australian nexus.
extractor_note: Radar item (published 15 Jul, one day before the 16-17 Jul window; <=7 days old). MHI is a solutions-provider OEM; org_type best-fit OG-major not ideal but closest listed for a large diversified industrial -> kept as stated.
